column33: Comments

Super unique setup! Round 1 didn't go great, mostly all I saw was Hand Ninjas and scheme Twists, so low vpr and high escapes. Round 2 went a little smoother, think I got real lucky with the villain decks

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Double Spiderman was the only way I saw out of this setup, or the fun level would be 1. Took out multiple tactics in one turn, with over 20 fight from chaining spidermen/sidekicks plus ultimate spidermans rare, but only 5 recruit was made. Luckily there was a henchmen in the city that gave me the last star for Nimrod. Escaped villians were just 3+ cost heroes I could KO in hopes for more 2 cost cards in the HQ. I fear the difference this pod will be from who drew Colossus of the future past and who drew schemes for that escape number.

Oh how I was glad I saved my Domino for later, without her those master strikes would have ended me. Both attempts where very similar, just got one stroke of luck in second one, where twist on top of deck (I knew its here because Rhino likes spoilers :D) was stopped by timely intervention of Colosus from out of time, so on theme. Mad respect for great setup

Had no idea at first how to make this works, but I figured Spider-Man would give me just enough Recruit (along with Ninjas) to hit Nimrod. One hand played Spiders and Magik's Rally New Mutants, and I was soon flooding with Sidekicks. I kicked Nimrod's butt 3 times in 1 turn! Second hand played Wolverines and tried attacking Villains, but ended up not doing much except cleaning HQ.
Was able to win both games but the first turn was very close to a loss. Got beyond lucky in the second game with the Skrull Queen giving me core Wolverine's rare which helped me to an easy victory.

Man oh man this was an insanely tough setup. Ended up winning by the skin of our teeth. The wolverine hand was focusing on cleaning the city spots while the thor/lady thor deck was working on nimrod. Finally was able to get an insane turn and draw enough attack to hit nimrod twice for the last tactic and final blow. Game 1 felt like we didn't have a chance to even play but Game 2 we were able to barely pull through.

I maintain that Fragmented Realities is the hardest Scheme (at least for 2 player), and the villain groups didn't make it any easier. Add in Nimrod and 3 forced heroes which have a grand total of *0* stars??? Oof. First attempt went horribly, I picked Lady Thor and Captain Marvel because they work well together and generate lots of stars, but the colors didn't gel and they were too slow, so I lost to the scheme. 2nd attempt, I went for two other of my favorite heroes, Iron Fist and Domino, because they can also generate lots of money, but the attack rewards are a lot faster (I wish I could've used Proxima Midnight, but I used her last week!). One side went double Wolverine with yellow from Domino, and the other side went Magik/Domino/Iron Fist. Went wonderfully. The last turn got 77 attack thanks to a helpful tactic which drew Living Weapon, which then caused a sidekick chain, which then bought and drew Berserker Rage and two Dimensional Portals. Challenging setup, happy to have beaten it! Still got too close to losing (9 heroes in the KO pile, and one Scheme Twist left)

My first game, I got ruined. Have you ever had 3 escapes on turn 3? 16 turns, 3 twists, and that was all she wrote. It was brutal.

Second game went much better, obviously! If I had to pick one card for MVP it was Battlefield Promotion from Fury. Got one on each side early, and helped me get to the 6 recruit needed for Nimrod without adding many actual recruit cards. Player 1 built a Wolverine draw deck with Magik's Rally the New Mutants for sidekicks, which was very effective in keeping the realities clear. Player 2 grabbed Fury and Forge cards to negate Master Strikes and build attack. By the time I got to turn 18, each side was organically generating enough recruit due to the battlefield promotions, I was able to unlock attacks on Nimrod. Funny enough, I never saw either Forge or Fury's ultimate in either game. The free defeat of the MM from either would have been nice. 

There were a couple of touch and go moments where I didn't have enough atk to clear a reality space, and had several escapes. There were 7 heroes in the KO pile at the end of the game for me, because one of the escapes was Skrull Queen Veranke. 

This was not great fun, however. The pressure of having to worry about playing multiple cards from the villain deck without being able to do anything about it is not a great feeling. I'm legitimately stunned that I was able to win this setup, especially with how the first game went. Kudos, though, to the theme and difficulty!

Our first game was such a disaster I didn't think we'd have a chance to win the scheme, let alone worry about points. This scheme is just so brutal in tournament format - every escape was scary for points and losing the scheme. 

We picked Domino and Forge for the Tech - synergy with each other for Versatile, some recruit and defense against the master strike. We did get to pull off BFG once which was satisfying. However probably we over-thought the fear for the master strike. In both games I think once of us actually had a tech card once to actually defend. 

The MVP was Wolverine X-Force's KO card which we got early the second game and helped clean my deck. Combined with Sidekicks and normal Wolverine drawing extra cards, was pulling this KO nearly every other turn.

column2: Blackjack Vulture in the Dead of Night
column33: We won the first and last twist that came out, but the two EditedMist won allowed him to get both Shuri uncommons and with a sunspot common, he was able to control the HQ.Tooms gave him one henchmen for his rage factory. Wild

Danger Sense 3 is very powerful. I used those to dig up 2 schemes and bury 1 master strike. I get 2 damage at least every time I used it, took out some henchmen for that extra card draw, and I own both blackjack games, gaining me 2 3 attack heroes both times. Solid game and I couldn't believe I won that fast

First game was a lost ( very unlucky with the scheme twists, only winning two and the next four was a lose) Also the Master Strikes compound very quickly, making it very difficult to hit Adrian Toomes. On my second attempt, I decided to knock out Adrian as quickly as possible, and sure enough I had a great combo with Liz and Michelle (Peter Allies) for the star hits on right hand; left hand I was able to get "Avenger in training" combo with claws and lucky danger sense draws. Combined for a very fast 13 round knock out against Toomes! My blackjack draws were also better, winning 2 hands and one lose to acquire those key combo cards.
My "opponent" should go to Vegas.  Really had to push my luck to not get beat.  Destroyed me on my second game.
Brought Peters Allies to hopefully build a quick recruit deck and hit the MM with Michelle, which worked rather well. White Wolf was the back up plan to wound everything!!
Playing Blackjack with the mastermind was pretty fun! MVP for this game is Michelle! Being able to hit the Mastermind with recruit was *chefs kiss*

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: I lost the first three scheme twists playing blackjack (21-20, 18-18, bust) so I was on high stress from there on out. Made extensive use of danger sense from high tech spider-man to manage future scheme twists because of how scary they were. Won the next three twists, but had to make some risky "hits". I didn't get a single master strike, so Adrian Toomes stayed at 5 attack the whole game. Could have won faster, but I would have had escapes, so I balanced those together and hope I made the correct call. Managed to keep moonlight most of the time which helped Werewolf by Night and Wolfsbane to work well. Those two are really a fantastic combo I recommend that everyone try out. Overall, things went about as smoothly as they could other than those first 3 twists putting me at death's door. Heroes synergized well and the scheme was very fun.
Spider-man, Spider-man, Does whatever a spider can. Turns out one of those is proccing ambushes on King Black Panther
There's little that's more satisfying in all of Legendary than winning Blackjack against the Mastermind

column28: Wong, Master of the Mystic Arts
column30: Wong, Master of the Mystic Arts

A Mastermind tactic added a Henchmen to a victory pile right from the villain deck, accommodating for a missing card. 

That aside, Wong and Tony Stark worked okay for me, leaving me a bit nervous but getting me there at the end. Had some close calls, and was fortunate with the scheme twists!

column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: One side focused on recruit cards and could shatter Exodus down to 1 or 2 attack.  The other side took care of the city. I don't think I did enough to move on, but it has been a great tournament.  Good luck in the next pods to everyone.

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: Ramp-ramp-ramp!

Luke Cage Noir was an obvious choice, with every other required hero card already being 4+ in cost. There was enough yellow and blue that I thought I could make Shatterstar work. Sadly, he really never showed up. But Luke Cage Noir was an absolute beast!

I wanted lots of draw and some cards that could scale to defeat Exodus, Mr. Fantastic and Luke Cage Noir do both of those things. Weight of the World and One Gigantic Hand fortunately both came up so I got the win. Wouldn't be surprised if someone had a faster win  with more points per turn though. I didn't see any copies of follow big leads either game.

Shatter creates so much math but is extremely fun to find the most efficient use of all card values. This set up was made for Luke Cage Noir. I put Strong Guy in to continue the 4 cost theme and had great synergy with Multiple Man. Slow start but accelerated very quickly once cards stared lining up.

Match 1 I used Strong Guy & Luke Cage Noir. My HQ got clogged early with expensive/Recruit cards while the city filled up with Sinister Clones and 4 Villains escaped. After that I was recovering, KOing well to thin my decks, but my first hit on the Mastermind led to hand full of grey for the other player, which coinciding with back-to-back Twists and another low Recruit hand that finished me off. Match 2, I took Siryn instead of Strong Guy and I had things much more under control. No escapes, and I only lost 2 Bystanders to the Scheme. I could not convince the HQ to give me any KO cards though. Disappearing Act pulled a Disappearing Act! This slowed me so much that I could not get the final blow before the Villain deck ran out. Still, I figure a no-escape draw is better than a loss! At least reporting what was left in my Villain deck was easy!
We had a lot of fun.  We were ravaged in the fist game with clones and twists.  We had a good bounce back in game two thanks to our first twists coming near the halfway point.

My first attempt was so close to being a win. I could draw two cards from an Acolyte's FIght Effect but drew a wound as the second draw. If that draw had been the top card at the end of the game instead I could have investigated for instinct or ranged, which would have given me Shatterstar's 3-cost which would have given me enough recruit to shatter Exodus an extra time and my attack total would have been his Final Blow. This setup felt really brutal, and at the start I even thought that my first attempt would be over really quickly because I ended up drawing 2 schemes back to back for the first 2 cards (I only 'saved/rescued/interrogated (what is the right term?)'  one hidden witness, so 2 bystanders still ended up in the escape pile). WIth enough bad luck that seemed like it could end the game before it really took off, but luckily it ended up not being a big problem for attempt 1 (or attempt 2). Attempt 1 made me switch heroes, as I noticed that 9 recruit was enough to make shatterstar a 4 attack enemy. Shatterstar and Rictor just didn't feel like they pulled their weight in the first attempt, but that was in hindsight only because Exodus is just a really hard mastermind. I chose to go for Luke Cage Noir and Thing in my second attempt, just hoping I could squeeze out enough recruit, which I did, but it turned out worse than attempt 1.

In attempt 1 I managed to clone and/or recruit all multiple men. The recruit and K.O. cards ended up in deck 1 and the investigate and attack 'ramp' cards in deck 2. For attempt 1 deck 2 turned out to be the support deck, not being able to get close to Exodus numbers and helping out with hidden witnesses and clearing the city. Deck 1 in attempt 1 was almost strong enough to win (as you can read above) and managed to defeat Exodus 4 times of which the pressure becaame intense for urns 31 and above, as these turns needed to be so good that I could defeat Exodus (almost) once every turn and unfortunately I came up short (Friggin' wound!) in turn 39.

Attempt 2 only had 38 turns, because Emma Frost managed to escape and investigate a scheme twist. In that attempt I managed to defeat Exodus twice with deck 1 and once with deck 2. I got recruit f*cked in that game too, just managing to clone Grant Ward for Deck 2 and a Maria Hill for Deck 1 with Mr. Sinister Clones. I had a LOT of turns where I had loads of recruit but no attack, which was terrible allround. Partly because of that instead of only 2 escpaes (as in attempt 1) 5 villains escaped in attempt 2.

Fun level was pretty much right  between middle and high, because it felt just tense enough that you could still believe in a win (at least in attempt 1), but I was just unlucky enough to not do so... Most of the acolytes were pretty defeatable, most escapes were due to the double filling of the city by Mr. Sinister Clones. Luke Cage's recruit 4 cost did only seem very strong on paper, in practice he managed to not do much. Maybe also because K.O.-ing starter cards was down to Multpile Man's Rare and Invisible Woman's Disappearing act (which didn't do much, as I mostly recruited other cards in her turns and/or chose to 'save/rescue/interrogate' hidden witnesses, to prevent a potential 'Evil Wins'-situation.

If enough people in pod 25 manage to defeat Exodus I think this was my swan song for this tournament, but perhaps they all struggle a bit as well (in which case I think I still have a chance). Thanks to Kylah for all the work behind the scenes. I can't imagine it being simple or easy to come up with so many setups in increasing difficulty every week on a tight schedule. I enjoyed my run so far and will hopefully have some time for the Randomizer League again soon. Peter, Out!

Wowzers that was rough. Maybe it was my hero choices I couldn't figure out if recruit or attack was more important and in the end I think I failed to do both. I did get much closer to winning though on game 2 with only 1 tactic and the FB left compared to having taken only 1 tactic on game 1.

column2: Symbiote in the Sky, I Can Go Twice as High
column33: We absolutely blew through this! a ton of synergy with the heroes we chose, got a lot of the bigger villains to kill, very fun!

column29: I did not play a second attempt.
column30: I did not play a second attempt.
column33: The melking continues
Was able to get BFG early and was sweating bullets during every feast. Punisher helped fight a few extra villains to give a slightly better score than the first game.

Forge was intended to meld with Mr. F and Domino. And aid in the destruction of the Phalanx. He did that serviceably. In attempt 1 he whipped out his you know what and got a free hit on Carnage to end the game. His you know what stayed hidden in attempt 2, but Domino peaked in attempt 2. Because of some twisting equations the last left hand of the game started with 9 cards, and ended up including all three specialized ammunitions, which allowed to clear the city and get the final blow in one turn, which was very hyped. 
Right hand wanted to go with the rest of the gang (Spider-Woman, Colossus, and Black Cat). Attempt one shed some light on a wicked combo of radioactive spider putting a high attack card on top of my deck, then pickpocketing it, then drawing it back with bioelectric shock. I did that with Cat Burglar (5 printed attack) twice, yielding 12 attack between the four cards every time, which allowed for some great turns. Attempt 2 didn't have smooth moves like attempt 1, but I managed to recruit the heavy tank and some silent statues, which just led to blunt force trauma to Carnage. Why draw many cards when few cards do trick?
All in all attempt 2 took less turns than attempt 1. I was super excited to hit Carnage for the first time on turn 14. The scheme was scary but didn't threaten any escapes so I got to focus on Carnage a lot. Similar with his Strike ability. I actually welcomed him deck thinning for me. Until he feasted on Cat burglar in attempt 2. Things got personal and two left turns later Domino avenged BC with emphasis. 
Fun setup! Hope it turns out well enough!

A fun group to race against the clock with, and lots of KO potential helped with Feast, the Villain groups (thanks double KO Moloids!), and the heroes I picked. Attempt 1 went well, getting Russian Heavy Tank early thanks to versatile, but Carnage feasted on it before I got to use it. 2nd attempt went much smoother, with the combo of Invisible Woman’s rare negating ambushes to draw 2 cards to consistently activating Wolverine’s Sudden Ambush for MM hits. VP is low, but hopefully speed helps here!

Great fun! In both of our games, by turn two, we had three ultimates fill the HQ, which was difficult to deal with. Also in both games, Carnage also feasted on some ultimates which definitely sucked. However, we were able to bounce back better on the second attempt. Human Torch paired well with Colossus and Spider-Woman paired well with Black Cat. And Mr. Fantastic and Domino cards filled in the space between each deck. The heroes were pretty rounded. One of us went wound gain heavy with Colossus and Human Torch and the other went Wall-Crawl heavy with Black Cat and Spider-Woman.

Forge was an obvious choice for me, and I was able to pull his rare in both games, leading to an early end to the 2nd game (18 turns). First game I went for the XF synergy, but Cable didn't really do anything for me and I got choked down on recruit and Black Cat cards. Second game I switched to Iron Fist to try and get synergy with Colossus' strength cards. Not sure if I should have submitted my first game (0 escape, 22 turns, 2.68 ppt) or second game (1 escape, 18 turns, 2.27 ppt). I feel like the turns are weighted much more than escapes, but this could have been a miscalculation.

We had some nice rounds. Easy scheme, low HP Mastermind. We chose cable, to "rush" the mastermind and get low on turns. Thing synergy with Mr. F was fantastic.
(Random) feast sucks. Over the second game we KO'd 30 cards, which costed 25 money and got legendary sniped 2 times. Black Cats Wall Crawl and top card reveal allowed us to control the Feasts a bit.

I thought my first game went well with 2 escapes and round 32, but my second run was insane! I managed to get Mr. Fantastic and human torch going (I felt angel wasn't synergizing with the rest as much as I'd hoped). Spider-woman is probably my favorite hero at this point with how much value she provides, and in the scenario she really shined with being able to get targeted feasts and some small synergies with the occasional blackcat sadness.  I managed to get the unstable molecules/one gigantic hand combo alongside HT's rare pretty early, and that cleared multiple masterminds.  

I love fighting carnage its such chaos and I love deckthinning its why I love marvel legendary there's so much deckthinning!!!

P2 had the Iron Man/ Doc Ock deck and was basically able to draw most of her deck every turn after getting it going. Fun game. Made me consider what I was missing by not using Villains as much.
